title: CoSparTA Pipeline Walkthrough
text: Raw data typically arrive long-format data frame one row per observed event. build_tensor() converts directly required n×p×wn \times p \times w integer count tensor single call, handling dimension labeling ensuring time bins retained even unobserved. resulting tensor dimensions 1000 × 100 × 50 sparsity approximately 0.97.
code: # Load the synthetic demo data df <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "demo_df.rds", package = "CoSparTA")) cov_df <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "demo_covariates.csv",                                  package = "CoSparTA")) channel_groups <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "demo_channel_groups.rds",                                         package = "CoSparTA"))  # Reconstruct the universe of dimension labels session_ids   <- sort(unique(df$session_id)) hour_labels   <- paste0("H", sprintf("%02d", 0:99)) channel_names <- c("Retail", "Social", "Review", "Deal", "Search") website_names <- as.vector(   outer(channel_names, paste0("_", sprintf("%02d", 1:10)), paste0) )  # Build 1000 x 100 x 50 tensor tensor_out <- build_tensor(   data         = df,   row          = "session_id",   col          = "hour",   slice        = "website",   value        = "count",   row_levels   = session_ids,   col_levels   = hour_labels,   slice_levels = website_names ) X <- tensor_out$X  # 1000 x 100 x 50 integer tensor  # Covariate matrix: two continuous covariates Xcov_mat <- as.matrix(cov_df[, c("cov1", "cov2")])

title: CoSparTA Pipeline Walkthrough
text: CoSparTA() main entry point. Supplying Xcov activates covariate-dependent priors observation mode, integrating contextual information directly decomposition rather post-hoc adjustment. rank KK reflects assumed number latent factors. optional CP-APR warm-start via init_cpapr() recommended Python available, provides better initialization random Gamma draws. covariate effects expected differ across factors, rank-specific covariate sets can supplied list. instance, factor 1 receives covariates, factor 2 left covariate-free, factors 3 4 receive different covariate: Missing entries handled CoSparTA_missing(), restricts likelihood observed entries:
code: # Optional CP-APR warm-start (requires Python with pyCP_APR) # python3 -m venv cosparta_env # source cosparta_env/bin/activate # pip install pyCP_APR numpy init_vals <- init_cpapr(X, K = 4, virtualenv = "cosparta_env")  # Covariate-aware fit fit <- CoSparTA(   X        = X,   K        = 4,   Xcov     = Xcov_mat,   init     = init_vals,   # or "random_gamma" if Python unavailable   maxiter  = 20,   convergence_criteria = "factor_change",   tol      = 1e-6,   verbose  = TRUE )  # Retrieve outputs via the unified getter U1     <- get_loadings(fit, "U1")      # 1000 x 4 normalized loadings U2     <- get_loadings(fit, "U2")      # 100  x 4 time factors U3     <- get_loadings(fit, "U3")      # 50   x 4 channel weights lambda <- get_loadings(fit, "weight")  # length-4 factor weights gamma  <- get_loadings(fit, "gamma")   # list of 4 covariate coefficient vectors Xcov_cov1 <- Xcov_mat[, 1, drop = FALSE] Xcov_cov2 <- Xcov_mat[, 2, drop = FALSE]  fit_rankspec <- CoSparTA(   X    = X,   K    = 4,   Xcov = list(Xcov_mat, NULL, Xcov_cov1, Xcov_cov2),   init = init_vals,   convergence_criteria = "factor_change" ) mask     <- generate_missing_mask(X, missing_rate = 0.10) fit_miss <- CoSparTA_missing(   X        = X,   K        = 4,   Xcov     = Xcov_mat,   obs_mask = mask$obs_mask,   convergence_criteria = "factor_change" )

title: CoSparTA Pipeline Walkthrough
text: variational posterior retains spike--slab structure prior, exact posterior credible intervals available closed form via get_posterior_quantile().
code: # Exact 95% posterior credible intervals for channel mode (U3) ci_w <- get_posterior_quantile(fit, probs = c(0.025, 0.975), mode = "W") # ci_w$q2.5, ci_w$q97.5  -- 50 x 4 lower and upper bounds  # Delta-method confidence intervals for covariate coefficients ci_gamma <- get_gamma_ci(fit, level = 0.95) # ci_gamma[[k]]$estimate, $se, $lower, $upper, $pvalue print(ci_gamma[[1]])  # factor 1 results

title: CoSparTA Pipeline Walkthrough
text: covariate set large, select_covariates() provides initial screening step. fits covariate-free model first, regresses observation-mode loadings covariates flags significant p<0.05p < 0.05 per factor. selected covariates can passed CoSparTA() via list interface shown Stage 2.
code: # Step 1: fit covariate-free model fit_unsup <- CoSparTA(   X                    = X,   K                    = 4,   Xcov                 = NULL,   init                 = init_vals,   convergence_criteria = "factor_change" )  # Step 2: screen covariates sel <- select_covariates(   K              = 4,   covariate_data = as.data.frame(Xcov_mat),   fit            = fit_unsup )  # sel$selected: list of selected covariate indices per factor print(sel$selected)

title: CoSparTA Pipeline Walkthrough
text: project_tensor() scores new observations onto estimated factor space without refitting model. reconstruct_tensor() returns denoised Poisson mean tensor μ̂\hat{\mu}.
code: # Project held-out sessions onto the factor space X_new <- X[991:1000, , ] F_new <- project_tensor(X_new, fit)  # 10 x 4 score matrix  # Reconstruct the denoised Poisson mean tensor X_hat <- reconstruct_tensor(fit)     # 1000 x 100 x 50

title: CoSparTA Pipeline Walkthrough
text: non-negative CP decomposition essentially unique (Kolda Bader 2009), extracted factor matrices carry direct physical interpretation — column represents genuinely distinct latent behavioral pattern. Two dedicated plotting functions produce ggplot2 figures time channel factor matrices:
code: # Faceted line plot of K time factors plot_time_factors(fit, time_labels = hour_labels)  # Faceted bar plot of K channel factors with category grouping plot_channel_factors(fit,                      channel_names  = website_names,                      channel_groups = channel_groups)

title: Covariate-Aware Sparse Tensor Analysis
text: Raw data arrive long-format data frame one row per observed event. build_tensor converts required n x p x w integer count tensor. Dimension labels can supplied explicitly ensure time bins retained even unobserved.
code: library(CoSparTA)  # Load bundled demo data df             <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "demo_df.rds", package = "CoSparTA")) data("demo_covariates", package = "CoSparTA"); cov_df <- demo_covariates channel_groups <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "demo_channel_groups.rds", package = "CoSparTA"))  Xcov_mat <- as.matrix(cov_df[, c("cov1", "cov2")])  # Reconstruct universe of labels session_ids   <- sort(unique(df$session_id)) hour_labels   <- paste0("H", sprintf("%02d", 0:99)) channel_names <- c("Retail", "Social", "Review", "Deal", "Search") website_names <- as.vector(   outer(channel_names, paste0("_", sprintf("%02d", 1:10)), paste0))  # Build 1000 x 100 x 50 tensor tensor_out <- build_tensor(   data         = df,   row          = "session_id",   col          = "hour",   slice        = "website",   value        = "count",   row_levels   = session_ids,   col_levels   = hour_labels,   slice_levels = website_names ) X <- tensor_out$X

title: Covariate-Aware Sparse Tensor Analysis
text: CoSparTA main entry point. Supplying Xcov activates covariate-dependent priors observation mode. optional CP-APR warm-start available via init_cpapr Python accessible. Rank-specific covariate sets missing data also supported:
code: # Optional CP-APR warm-start (requires Python 3.9+ with pyCP_APR) # use_virtualenv("cosparta_env", required = TRUE) init_vals <- tryCatch(   init_cpapr(X, K = 4, virtualenv = "cosparta_env"),   error = function(e) "random_gamma" )  fit <- CoSparTA(   X                    = X,   K                    = 4,   Xcov                 = Xcov_mat,   init                 = init_vals,   maxiter              = 20,   convergence_criteria = "factor_change",   tol                  = 1e-6,   verbose              = TRUE ) # Retrieve outputs via the unified getter: U1     <- get_loadings(fit, "U1")      # n x K normalized loadings U2     <- get_loadings(fit, "U2")      # p x K time factors U3     <- get_loadings(fit, "U3")      # w x K channel weights lambda <- get_loadings(fit, "weight")  # length-K component weights gamma  <- get_loadings(fit, "gamma")   # list of K covariate coefficient vectors # Rank-specific covariates fit_rankspec <- CoSparTA(X, K = 4,   Xcov = list(Xcov_mat, NULL, Xcov_mat[,1,drop=FALSE], Xcov_mat[,2,drop=FALSE]),   init = init_vals, convergence_criteria = "factor_change")  # Missing data mask     <- generate_missing_mask(X, missing_rate = 0.10) fit_miss <- CoSparTA_missing(X, K = 4, Xcov = Xcov_mat,                              obs_mask = mask$obs_mask,                              convergence_criteria = "factor_change")

what: Covariate-aware Empirical Bayes Tensor Decomposition — CoSparTA
title: Covariate-aware Empirical Bayes Tensor Decomposition — CoSparTA
text: Fits non-negative CP tensor decomposition using empirical Bayes priors spike--slab structure Poisson likelihood. covariates supplied via Xcov, enter generative model directly covariate-dependent Poisson rates form \(\lambda_i = \beta \cdot \exp(X_i^\top \gamma)\), enabling simultaneous decomposition covariate effect estimation. Without covariates (Xcov = NULL), method reduces unsupervised empirical Bayes Poisson tensor decomposition.

title: Covariate-aware Empirical Bayes Tensor Decomposition — CoSparTA
code: CoSparTA(   X,   K,   Xcov = NULL,   lib_size = NULL,   init = "random_gamma",   maxiter = 100,   maxiter_init = 100,   tol = 1e-06,   compute_elbo_final = FALSE,   ebpm.fn = c(ebpm_point_gamma_multiplier_covariates, ebps_with_uq,     ebpm_point_gamma_with_uq),   fix_L = FALSE,   fix_F = FALSE,   fix_W = FALSE,   smooth_F = T,   printevery = 10,   verbose = TRUE,   convergence_criteria = "factor_change",   n_stable = 3L,   U1_true = NULL,   U2_true = NULL,   U3_true = NULL )

title: Covariate-aware Empirical Bayes Tensor Decomposition — CoSparTA
text: X 3-dimensional non-negative integer array dimensions n x p x w, representing observations (e.g., users), time points, channels respectively. K Integer. Number components (CP rank) decomposition. Xcov Covariate input observation mode. Can : (1) NULL fully unsupervised decomposition; (2) numeric matrix dimension n x q, applied identically K components; (3) list length K, element either numeric covariate matrix (dimensions n x q_k, potentially different numbers covariates per component) NULL unsupervised components. Default NULL. lib_size Numeric vector length n giving per-observation library sizes (exposure/scaling factors). Default NULL, sets sizes 1. init Character string specifying initialization method. Default 'random_gamma'. maxiter Integer. Maximum number EM iterations. Default 100. maxiter_init Integer. Maximum number iterations initialization step. Default 100. tol Numeric. Convergence tolerance. Iterations stop change objective threshold. Default 1e-6. compute_elbo_final Logical. TRUE, computes ELBO final iteration even convergence_criteria != 'ELBO'. Default FALSE. ebpm.fn single function list three functions specifying empirical Bayes prior L, F, W modes respectively. Default uses ebpm_point_gamma_multiplier_covariates L (covariate-aware), ebps_with_uq F (smooth), ebpm_point_gamma_with_uq W (point-gamma), returning posterior variance PIP applicable. fix_L Logical. TRUE, observation-mode loadings held fixed initialization. Default FALSE. fix_F Logical. TRUE, time-mode factors held fixed. Default FALSE. fix_W Logical. TRUE, channel-mode weights held fixed. Default FALSE. smooth_F Logical. TRUE, applies smoothing time-mode factors. Default TRUE. printevery Integer. Number iterations progress messages verbose = TRUE. Default 10. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints initialization iteration progress. Default TRUE. convergence_criteria Character string specifying convergence criterion: 'factor_change' (maximum column-normalized change across three factor matrices, default), 'mKLabs' (mean KL divergence), 'ELBO' (evidence lower bound). 'factor_change' recommended: avoids O(npwK) ELBO computation typically converges fewer iterations. n_stable Integer. Number consecutive iterations factor change tol required declaring convergence. used convergence_criteria = 'factor_change'. Default 3. U1_true Optional matrix true observation-mode factors, used track reconstruction error simulation studies. Default NULL. U2_true Optional matrix true time-mode factors simulation evaluation. Default NULL. U3_true Optional matrix true channel-mode factors simulation evaluation. Default NULL.

title: Covariate-aware Empirical Bayes Tensor Decomposition — CoSparTA
text: named list following elements: elbo Final ELBO value computed last iteration. obj_trace Numeric vector objective values iteration. res List variational posterior summaries. Key fields:     res$ql$El — posterior mean loadings (n x K);     res$ql$Elogl — posterior log-mean loadings (n x K);     res$ql$Varl — posterior variance loadings (n x K);     res$ql$PIPl — posterior inclusion probabilities loadings     (n x K), giving \(P(\theta_i \neq 0 \mid x_i)\)     observation component;     res$qf$Ef — posterior mean time factors (p x K);     res$qf$Elogf — posterior log-mean time factors (p x K);     res$qw$Ew — posterior mean channel weights (w x K);     res$qw$Elogw — posterior log-mean channel weights (w x K). diff_U List three vectors recording per-iteration reconstruction     error relative U1_true, U2_true, U3_true.     meaningful true factors supplied. run_time Elapsed computation time difftime object. addition, following normalized reordered fields populated automatically (columns scaled unit Frobenius norm, ordered descending \(\lambda\)): res$lambda_normed Numeric vector length K: component     weights \(\lambda_k = \|l_k\|\|f_k\|\|w_k\|\), sorted descending. res$gl_normed List K gamma estimates reordered descending     \(\lambda\). res$ql$El_normed Unit-norm observation loading matrix     (n x K), reordered descending \(\lambda\). res$ql$PIPl_normed PIP matrix L mode, reordered. res$ql$shape_post_l_normed Posterior gamma shape L, reordered. res$ql$rate_post_l_normed Posterior gamma rate L scaled     \(\|l_k\|\), reordered, \(El\_normed[,k] \sim     \text{Gamma}(\text{shape},\, \text{rate\_normed})\) marginally. res$qf$Ef_normed, res$qf$PIPf_normed, res$qf$shape_post_f_normed,     res$qf$rate_post_f_normed pattern F mode. res$qw$Ew_normed, res$qw$PIPw_normed, res$qw$shape_post_w_normed,     res$qw$rate_post_w_normed pattern W mode.

title: Covariate-aware Empirical Bayes Tensor Decomposition — CoSparTA
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(42) X <- array(rpois(20 * 12 * 4, lambda = 1.5), dim = c(20, 12, 4)) Xcov <- matrix(rnorm(20 * 2), nrow = 20, ncol = 2)  # Supervised decomposition with covariates fit <- CoSparTA(X, K = 3, Xcov = Xcov, maxiter = 50, verbose = FALSE) EL <- fit$res$ql$El   # 20 x 3 loading matrix EF <- fit$res$qf$Ef   # 12 x 3 factor matrix EW <- fit$res$qw$Ew   # 4 x 3 weight matrix  # Unsupervised decomposition (no covariates) fit0 <- CoSparTA(X, K = 3, Xcov = NULL, maxiter = 50, verbose = FALSE) } # }

text: X 3-dimensional non-negative integer array dimensions n x p x w. May contain NA missing entries. K Integer. Number components (CP rank). Xcov Covariate input observation mode. title: Build a 3-Way Tensor from Long-Format Data — build_tensor
text: data data frame long format. row Character string. Column name mode 1 (e.g., users/sessions). col Character string. Column name mode 2 (e.g., time). slice Character string. Column name mode 3 (e.g., channels). value Character string NULL. Column name containing counts values. NULL, row treated single event occurrences counted per (row, col, slice) combination. Default NULL. time_bins Controls time binning col dimension: NULL binning. Treats col categorical;     unique value becomes time index. Default. Integer Number equal-width bins. Works numeric     POSIXct/POSIXlt datetime columns. Numeric vector Explicit break points cut().     Must cover full range col column. fill Numeric. Value fill unobserved (row, col, slice) combinations. Default 0L. row_levels Optional character factor vector specifying complete set levels row dimension (e.g. session IDs). NULL, levels inferred data. col_levels Optional character factor vector specifying complete set levels column dimension (e.g. time slots). NULL, levels inferred data. Recommended time dimensions empty slots preserved. slice_levels Optional character factor vector specifying complete set levels slice dimension (e.g. channels/websites). NULL, levels inferred data.

title: Build a 3-Way Tensor from Long-Format Data — build_tensor
text: function handles following time column types: Character factor Treated categorical. Sorted     alphabetically unless already factor levels. Numeric integer time_bins integer, binned     equal-width intervals via cut(). POSIXct POSIXlt time_bins integer,     time range divided equal-width intervals. title: Build a 3-Way Tensor from Long-Format Data — build_tensor
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Raw event log: count events per (user, time_bin, channel) events <- data.frame(   user = rep(paste0("u", 1:10), each = 50),   timestamp = runif(500, 0, 600),   channel = sample(c("view", "click", "purchase"), 500, replace = TRUE) ) tensor_data <- build_tensor(events, row = "user", col = "timestamp",                              slice = "channel", time_bins = 12) dim(tensor_data$X)       # 10 x 12 x 3 tensor_data$dim2_labels  # bin labels  # Pre-aggregated monthly data monthly <- data.frame(   user = rep(paste0("u", 1:5), each = 12),   month = rep(month.abb, 5),   channel = sample(c("web", "app"), 60, replace = TRUE),   count = rpois(60, 3) ) tensor_data <- build_tensor(monthly, row = "user", col = "month",                              slice = "channel", value = "count")  # Datetime timestamps with explicit bin breaks events_dt <- data.frame(   session = rep(1:20, each = 30),   time = as.POSIXct("2024-01-01") + runif(600, 0, 86400),   action = sample(c("browse", "cart", "buy"), 600, replace = TRUE) ) tensor_data <- build_tensor(events_dt, row = "session", col = "time",                              slice = "action", time_bins = 24)  # Feed directly into CoSparTA fit <- CoSparTA(tensor_data$X, K = 3)  # Ensure all 48 time slots are present even if some are empty tensor_out <- build_tensor(   data       = df,   row        = "session_id",   col        = "hour",   slice      = "website",   value      = "count",   col_levels = paste0("H", sprintf("%02d", 0:47)) ) } # }

what: Empirical Bayes Poisson Mean Estimation with Covariate-Dependent Rates — ebpm_point_gamma_multiplier_covariates
title: Empirical Bayes Poisson Mean Estimation with Covariate-Dependent Rates — ebpm_point_gamma_multiplier_covariates
text: Fits spike--slab point-gamma prior vector counts x Poisson rate modulated individual-level covariates. effective rate observation \(\) \(\lambda_i = \beta \cdot \exp(X_i^\top \gamma)\), prior mean scales multiplicatively covariates. Prior parameters \((\pi_0, \alpha, \beta, \gamma)\) estimated maximizing marginal likelihood via nlm (optim fallback). function core building block covariate-aware mode update CoSparTA.

title: Empirical Bayes Poisson Mean Estimation with Covariate-Dependent Rates — ebpm_point_gamma_multiplier_covariates

title: Empirical Bayes Poisson Mean Estimation with Covariate-Dependent Rates — ebpm_point_gamma_multiplier_covariates
text: x Non-negative integer vector observed counts. s Numeric scalar vector length length(x) giving per-observation exposure/scaling factors. Default 1. X Numeric covariate matrix dimension n x q, rows correspond observations x columns covariates. g_init Optional numeric vector starting values order c(pi0, alpha, beta, gamma_1, ..., gamma_q). NULL, initialization uses baseline ebpm::ebpm_point_gamma fit gamma = 0. Default NULL. control Optional list control arguments passed nlm. Defaults: stepmax = 1, gradtol = 1e-6, steptol = 1e-8, iterlim = 1000, check.analyticals = FALSE.

title: Empirical Bayes Poisson Mean Estimation with Covariate-Dependent Rates — ebpm_point_gamma_multiplier_covariates
text: named list : fitted_g List estimated prior parameters:     pi0 — spike (point-mass zero) probability;     shape — gamma shape parameter \(\alpha\);     scale — gamma scale parameter \(1/\beta\);     gamma — covariate coefficient vector length q;     type — always "covariate_dependent". posterior Data frame one row per observation columns:     mean — posterior mean \(E[\theta_i \mid x_i]\);     mean_log — posterior log-mean \(E[\log\theta_i \mid x_i]\)     (-Inf observations \(x_i = 0\));     var — posterior variance     \(\text{Var}(\theta_i \mid x_i)\), decomposed within-component     variance (uncertainty gamma component) plus -component     variance (uncertainty whether spike fired);     pip — posterior inclusion probability     \(P(\theta_i \neq 0 \mid x_i) = 1 - \hat{\pi}_i\), giving     probability observation \(\) truly non-zero loading;     shape_post — posterior gamma shape parameter     \(\alpha + x_i\), shape gamma kernel non-spike     component; rate_post — posterior gamma rate parameter     \(\beta / \lambda_i + s_i\), rate gamma kernel. log_likelihood Maximized marginal log-likelihood. convergence_code Optimizer convergence code. nlm:     1–2 indicate convergence; 3–5 indicate potential issues (see     ?nlm). optim fallback: 0 = converged.

title: Empirical Bayes Poisson Mean Estimation with Covariate-Dependent Rates — ebpm_point_gamma_multiplier_covariates
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) n <- 200 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * 2), nrow = n) true_gamma <- c(0.4, -0.3) x <- rpois(n, lambda = exp(0.5 + X %*% true_gamma))  fit <- ebpm_point_gamma_multiplier_covariates(x, s = 1, X = X) fit$fitted_g$gamma     # estimated covariate coefficients fit$fitted_g$pi0       # estimated spike probability head(fit$posterior)    # posterior means and log-means } # }

what: Confidence Intervals for Covariate Coefficients — get_gamma_ci
title: Confidence Intervals for Covariate Coefficients — get_gamma_ci
text: Computes confidence intervals covariate effect parameters \(\gamma\) estimated CoSparTA. Two methods available: "delta" Fast asymptotic intervals based Hessian     marginal log-likelihood EBPM optimization. full     Hessian (covering \(\gamma\) together nuisance parameters     \(\pi_0\), \(\alpha\), \(\beta\)) inverted first,     \(\gamma\) submatrix extracted, implementing     \(\mathrm{Var}(\hat\gamma_k) = [H_k^{-1}]_{\gamma,\gamma}\) per     equation (21) paper. correctly accounts uncertainty     nuisance parameters. conditional standard errors     – measure uncertainty \(\gamma\) given current F W     estimates, may underestimate true uncertainty. Recommended     exploratory screening. "bootstrap" Parametric bootstrap intervals. Generates     B synthetic tensors fitted Poisson rates, refits     CoSparTA , collects bootstrap distribution     \(\gamma\). correctly accounts sources estimation     uncertainty (including uncertainty F W). Recommended     publication-quality inference. Computationally expensive: requires     B full model refits.

title: Confidence Intervals for Covariate Coefficients — get_gamma_ci
code: get_gamma_ci(   fit,   method = "delta",   level = 0.95,   B = 200,   X = NULL,   K = NULL,   Xcov = NULL,   normalized = TRUE,   init_fn = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Confidence Intervals for Covariate Coefficients — get_gamma_ci
text: fit fitted object returned CoSparTA. method Character: "delta" "bootstrap". Default "delta". level Numeric confidence level (0, 1). Default 0.95. B Integer. Number bootstrap replicates (used method = "bootstrap"). Default 200. X original tensor used fit model (required method = "bootstrap"). K Integer. Number components (required bootstrap). Xcov Covariate matrix list used original fit (required bootstrap). normalized Logical. TRUE (default), reads gamma estimates fit$res$gl_normed (reordered descending \(\lambda\)). FALSE, reads fit$res$gl (original component order). init_fn Optional function signature function(X_star, K) returns initialization object (e.g., list three matrices init_cpapr). provided, function called bootstrap replicate generate fresh initialization. NULL, init argument ... reused replicates. Using per-replicate initialization (e.g., via init_cpapr) recommended publication-quality bootstrap inference. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints bootstrap progress. Default TRUE. ... Additional arguments passed CoSparTA bootstrap refitting (e.g., maxiter, init, convergence_criteria).

title: Confidence Intervals for Covariate Coefficients — get_gamma_ci
text: list length K (one element per component). element   named list : estimate Numeric vector gamma estimates original fit. se Standard errors. lower Lower confidence bound. upper Upper confidence bound. pvalue Two-sided p-values (Wald test delta method;     bootstrap percentile-based bootstrap, computed     2 * min(prop >= 0, prop <= 0)). method Character string indicating method used. component fitted without covariates (unsupervised rank), entry NULL.

what: Compute Exact Posterior Quantiles from a CoSparTA fit — get_posterior_quantile
title: Compute Exact Posterior Quantiles from a CoSparTA fit — get_posterior_quantile
text: Computes exact quantiles marginal posterior distribution factor element. Dispatches posterior family tag stored fit object (fit$res$q*$family_*) user-specified ebpm.fn configurations produce correct intervals regardless prior family: "point_gamma" Spike--slab mixture Gamma slab.     marginal CDF \(F(\theta) = \hat\pi_i \cdot \mathbf{1}[\theta     \geq 0] + (1 - \hat\pi_i) \cdot F_{\text{Gamma}}(\theta)\),     quantile \(\tau\) 0 \(\tau \leq \hat\pi_i\)     \(F_{\text{Gamma}}^{-1}((\tau - \hat\pi_i)/(1-\hat\pi_i))\) otherwise. "smooth_lognormal" Log-normal posterior smooth     wavelet-based prior. Quantiles computed     \(\exp(\mu_{\log} + \sqrt{\sigma^2_{\log}} \cdot     \Phi^{-1}(\tau))\), \(\mu_{\log}\)     \(\sigma^2_{\log}\) posterior mean variance log space.     \(\sigma^2_{\log}\) unavailable, delta-method approximation     \(\sigma^2_{\log} \approx \text{Var}/\text{Mean}^2\) used. family tag absent (fit produced change), function falls back "point_gamma" warning.

what: Normalize and Sort Components of a CoSparTA fit — normalize_factors
title: Normalize and Sort Components of a CoSparTA fit — normalize_factors
text: Extracts factor matrices fitted CoSparTA object, normalizes column unit Frobenius norm, computes per-component weight \(\lambda_k = \|\mathbf{l}_k\| \|\mathbf{f}_k\| \|\mathbf{w}_k\|\), returns components sorted \(\lambda\) descending. canonical form comparing decompositions downstream use project_tensor reconstruct_tensor. Since CoSparTA now produces normalized outputs directly _normed fields (e.g. fit$res$ql$El_normed), function primarily useful legacy code custom normalization workflows applied fit objects lack fields.

Ew Normalized reordered channel weight matrix     (w x K). column unit Frobenius norm. lambda Numeric vector length K giving component     weights \(\lambda_k = \|\mathbf{l}_k\| \|\mathbf{f}_k\| \|\mathbf{w}_k\|\),     sorted descending. order Integer vector length K giving permutation     original component indices new (descending \(\lambda\)) order. gamma_list list length K. element gamma     coefficient vector component reordering descending     lambda. NULL components gamma estimates (.e.     model fit without covariates). lambda_order Integer vector length K giving     permutation used reorder components descending lambda. Useful     reordering component-indexed objects (e.g. Xcov_list)     match normalized factor ordering.

what: Project a New Tensor onto the Learned Factor Space — project_tensor
title: Project a New Tensor onto the Learned Factor Space — project_tensor
text: Given fitted CoSparTA object (raw factor matrices), projects one new observations onto learned factor space produce loading matrix. observation p x w count slice; function recovers K-dimensional representation without refitting. projection observation \(\) component \(k\) : $$F_{ik} = \lambda_k \, \mathbf{f}_k^\top X_{\text{new}[,,]} \, \mathbf{w}_k$$ normalize = TRUE raw matrices lambda supplied, $$F_{ik} = \mathbf{f}_k^\top X_{\text{new}[,,]} \, \mathbf{w}_k$$ using raw posterior means normalize = FALSE. Computation vectorized observations: X_new reshaped n_new x (p*w) matrix Kronecker factor \(\mathbf{f}_k \otimes \mathbf{w}_k\) p*w-vector, full projection component \(k\) reduces single matrix-vector multiply.

title: Project a New Tensor onto the Learned Factor Space — project_tensor

title: Project a New Tensor onto the Learned Factor Space — project_tensor
text: X_new Either 3D array dimensions n_new x p x w (multiple new observations), matrix dimensions p x w (single observation). latter case input wrapped array(X_new, dim = c(1, p, w)) length-K vector returned instead 1 x K matrix. fit fitted object returned CoSparTA CoSparTA_missing. Either fit Ef, Ew, lambda must supplied. normalize Logical. TRUE (default) fit supplied, calls normalize_factors factor columns unit Frobenius norm ordered \(\lambda\) descending. FALSE, uses raw posterior means scaling reordering. Ignored Ef, Ew, lambda provided directly. Ef Numeric matrix dimensions p x K (time factor matrix, unit-norm columns). Provide together Ew lambda bypass fit object. Ew Numeric matrix dimensions w x K (channel factor matrix, unit-norm columns). Provide together Ef lambda bypass fit object. lambda Numeric vector length K (component weights). Provide together Ef Ew bypass fit object.

title: Project a New Tensor onto the Learned Factor Space — project_tensor
text: X_new 3D array: n_new x K numeric matrix   projected loadings. X_new p x w matrix:   numeric vector length K.

what: Reconstruct the Denoised Mean Tensor from a CoSparTA fit — reconstruct_tensor
title: Reconstruct the Denoised Mean Tensor from a CoSparTA fit — reconstruct_tensor
text: Reconstructs denoised Poisson mean tensor \(\hat{X}[,j,m] = \sum_{k=1}^{K} L_{ik} F_{jk} W_{mk}\) raw (unnormalized) posterior mean factor matrices stored fitted CoSparTA object. result best rank-K approximation observed tensor fitted model. Implementation uses loop K components. \(k\) outer product \(\mathbf{l}_k \otimes \mathbf{f}_k \otimes \mathbf{w}_k\) added accumulator via tcrossprod sweep, avoiding allocation K separate n x p x w arrays.
